Bash(cat > /Users/dimidev32/001-projects/010-termlink/.context/episodic/T-079.yaml << 'YAMLEOF'
id: T-079
name: "Capability token system — HMAC-signed scope tokens"
workflow_type: inception
duration_days: 1
outcome: completed
decision: GO

summary: >
  Inception task exploring capability token design for Phase 3 security.
  Evaluated 3 approaches: \(A\) request-level tokens, \(B\) connection-level tokens,
  \(C\) hybrid. Chose Option B — connection-level auth via auth.token RPC method
  that upgrades scope for the connection lifetime. This fits the existing
  handle_connection\(scope\) parameter pattern with minimal changes.
  GO decision decomposed into 3 build tasks: T-086 \(token gen/validation\),
  T-087 \(server-side auth.token\), T-088 \(CLI token commands\).

key_decisions:
  - decision: "Connection-level token auth \(Option B\)"
    rationale: "Fits existing handle_connection\(scope\) pattern; one auth call per connection vs per-request overhead"
  - decision: "HMAC-SHA256 with base64\(payload\).base64\(signature\) format"
    rationale: "Stateless verification, no database needed, inspectable payload"
  - decision: "Backward compatibility — sessions without token_secret get legacy Execute scope"
    rationale: "Opt-in security avoids breaking existing sessions"

artifacts:
  - docs/reports/T-079-capability-tokens.md
  - .tasks/completed/T-079-capability-token-system--hmac-signed-sco.md

patterns: []
learnings:
  - "Inception to GO to 3 build tasks pattern works well for security features"
  - "Connection-level auth is simpler than request-level when the transport already carries scope"
YAMLEOF)

Bash(TRANSCRIPT="/Users/dimidev32/.claude/projects/-Users-dimidev32-001-projects-010-termlink/2392cce9-c6ff-430c-986c-91aef0c67688.jsonl"
python3 -c "
import json
total = 0
user_turns = 0
asst_turns = 0
tool_calls = 0
errors = 0
first_ts = None
last_ts = None
with open\('$TRANSCRIPT'\) as f:
    for line in f:
        try:
            e = json.loads\(line\)
            total += 1
            t = e.get\('type'\)
            ts = e.get\('timestamp'\)
            if ts:
                if not first_ts: first_ts = ts
                last_ts = ts
            if t == 'user':
                c = e.get\('message',{}\).get\('content',[]\)
                if isinstance\(c, list\):
                    for item in c:
                        if isinstance\(item,dict\):
                            if item.get\('type'\) == 'tool_result': tool_calls += 1
                            if item.get\('is_error'\): errors += 1
                elif isinstance\(c, str\) and len\(c\) > 5:
                    user_turns += 1
            elif t == 'assistant':
                asst_turns += 1
        except: pass
print\(f'Total lines: {total}'\)
print\(f'User turns: {user_turns}'\)
print\(f'Assistant turns: {asst_turns}'\)
print\(f'Tool results \(incl errors\): {tool_calls}'\)
print\(f'Errors: {errors}'\)
print\(f'First timestamp: {first_ts}'\)
print\(f'Last timestamp: {last_ts}'\)
")

  - id: G-005
    title: "Pure conversation sessions bypass all framework enforcement"
    severity: medium
    directive: D1-Antifragility
    status: watching
    discovered: "2026-03-11"
    discovered_by: "T-094 \(5-agent investigation\)"
    evidence: "Claude Code fires zero hooks for pure conversation sessions — no PreToolUse, no PostToolUse, no SessionEnd. The task gate, commit cadence enforcement, and session capture checklist only trigger on tool use. A session that never touches a file can run indefinitely and lose all content on context exhaustion."
    impact: "Research and exploration conversations — often the most valuable sessions — are silently ephemeral. No structural backstop exists. Agent self-governance is the only mitigation."
    mitigation: "C-002 Exploratory Conversation Guard \(CLAUDE.md rule\): stop at 3 substantive exchanges, create inception task, invoke /capture. /capture skill \(T-108\) provides the save mechanism."
    related_tasks: [T-094, T-095, T-108]
    report: "docs/reports/T-094-volatile-conversation-prevention.md"
    trigger_check:
      type: manual
      description: "Closes if Anthropic adds PostMessage or SessionEnd hooks to Claude Code \(T-099 PR request\)"
EOF)

# T-138: /self-test framework skill — E2E validation loop

## Context

Phase 2 from T-136 inception. Depends on T-137 \(`termlink interact`\).
Creates a `/self-test` skill for Claude Code that automates:

1. Spawn test session \(`termlink register --name self-test --shell`\)
2. Run a configurable sequence of framework commands via `termlink interact`
3. Collect pass/fail results per command
4. Report structured results to the agent
5. Clean up session

## Acceptance Criteria

### Agent
- [ ] `/self-test` skill created in framework skill directory
- [ ] Runs configurable command sequence \(default: `fw doctor`, `fw audit`\)
- [ ] Reports pass/fail per command with output excerpts
- [ ] Auto-spawns and cleans up TermLink test session
- [ ] Handles timeout and session failure gracefully

## Verification

# Skill file exists
test -f agents/self-test/self-test.sh || test -f .claude/skills/self-test.md

## Updates

# T-139: query.output --strip-ansi option

## Context

T-136 spike found that scrollback contains raw ANSI escape sequences which
break JSON parsers and make pattern matching harder. Add a `strip_ansi` param
to the `query.output` RPC handler that strips escape codes server-side.

## Acceptance Criteria

### Agent
- [ ] `query.output` RPC accepts optional `strip_ansi: true` param
- [ ] When set, output has ANSI escape sequences removed before returning
- [ ] `termlink output <session> --strip-ansi` CLI flag
- [ ] Backward compatible — default behavior unchanged
- [ ] Tests for ANSI stripping \(at least 2\)
- [ ] All existing tests pass

## Verification

/Users/dimidev32/.cargo/bin/cargo test --workspace 2>&1 | grep -q "test result: ok"
